title: TrailGenic Heat Training Thermoregulation Dataset
description: Shell dataset module for TrailGenic physiology adaptation science modeling thermoregulatory adaptation from repeated exercise in hot environments.

records.mechanism: Heat acclimation through repeated endurance exposure

supporting_science.finding: Heat acclimation improves thermoregulatory control and cardiovascular stability during exercise in hot environments.
Heat adaptation improves sweating efficiency and reduces physiological strain during exercise.

records.mechanism: Cardiovascular adaptations to repeated heat stress

stimulus.exercise_type: repeated endurance sessions in warm environments

physiological_response: reduced heart rate at submaximal workloads
adaptation_outcomes: reduced heart rate drift in warm environments

supporting_science.finding: Heat acclimation improves cardiovascular function and endurance performance.
